title: Pi-Context May 29 2026 LLM Perspective
summary: Earlier perspective doc enumerating 11 customization surfaces (registries the in-pi LLM expects to drive) that pi-context exposes — read-config / write-schema / amend-config / write-block / migrations + 6 others. Cited in FGAP-136 as the source of the 11-registry in-pi customization promise. Established that the in-pi customization should extend to schema-evolution-with-data (which FGAP-136 closed via write-schema-migration tool).
key_claims: Pi-context exposes 11 customization-registry surfaces accessible to the in-pi LLM via tool dispatch
Schema-evolution with existing data requires migration-tool surface (gap subsequently closed by FGAP-136)
Customization-as-tool-driven is the canonical pattern downstream consumers should be able to rely on
scope: Pi-context registry surfaces from the in-pi LLM perspective; what customization the LLM can drive via tools
